author: nflaig
content:
https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/624 cc <@808969530608451584> <@577788234810130432> (@Bharath 😢 )

I was thinking how we can rollout this change without breaking stuff, so from what I can see if buildoor sends `Eth-Blob-Data-Included: true` it should be fine to work with clients that implemented #624 and the ones that didn't

I wanna double check if I missed something else, but afaik buildoor produces the full payload outside and even the bid outside the beacon node, right?

author: nflaig
content:
I also added this note to the spec
```
   - If the payload was constructed outside the beacon node: sign envelope and [submit `SignedExecutionPayloadEnvelopeContents`](#/Beacon/publishExecutionPayloadEnvelope) (envelope + blobs + KZG proofs) via any beacon node
```
in `validator-flow.md` which is what buildoor does, I guess a bit misplaces in that file, but can think about moving that later

author: nflaig
content: you also will need to keep sending `Eth-Execution-Payload-Blinded: false` for pre-624 beacon nodes

author: pk910
content:
is it only the header? the rest seems to be the same as before for the stateless submission path.
so basically this line:  https://github.com/ethpandaops/go-eth2-client/blob/master/http/submitexecutionpayloadenvelope.go#L142

author: nflaig
content: yep, my pr pretty much changes the stateful flow which buildoor doesn't use right now

author: nflaig
content: so you can keep L142 for now and add the other header

author: pk910
content:
yea,   buildoor requestsa payloads directly via the engine api, so the bn won't know about the payload or included blobs for sure.
might be worth adding a second payload building path via the beacon node 🤔

author: nflaig
content: yes I think I wanna add that

author: nflaig
content: buildoor also construct the bids outside the bn right?

author: pk910
content: yes

author: nflaig
content: I feel like we need a /builder namespace on hte beacon api

author: pk910
content:
is there anything special in the bid?
it'S fully derived from the payload, no?

author: nflaig
content: yeah you can do a stateful flow as builder too

author: nflaig
content: but the current api spec doesn't allow it

author: nflaig
content: we have https://ethereum.github.io/beacon-APIs/?urls.primaryName=dev#/Validator/getExecutionPayloadBid, it's part of `/validator` namespace, but that doesn't make much sense imo

author: pk910
content:
dropping off blobs makes sense for the submission to drop that large amount of data from the hot path.
not sure if a fully stateful block production & bid creation makes sense..  what's the benefit of enshrining this into the api?

author: nflaig
content: how you wanna drop the blob submission though?

author: nflaig
content: somehow the builder needs to submit it to the beacon node, I am not sure if the fully stateful flow is worth it, it's a different way to design a builder, moves work to the beacon node

author: nflaig
content: but we do have `GET /eth/v1/validator/execution_payload_bids/{slot}/{builder_index}` already

author: nflaig
content: I think this is good for buildoor, when talking to Bharath about this on many occassions, his goal was always to make buildoor as close to how builders like titan or others would work on mainnet so we can find gaps in the specs etc.

author: nflaig
content: I am guessing builders on mainnet later want the stateless flow so they can submit to multiple bns, the extra overhead for them might be negligible, I do expect them to use `GET /eth/v1/beacon/execution_payload_envelopes` on the beacon node so they can rely on the beacon node for `broadcast_validation=consensus_and_equivocation` to detect proposer equivocations

author: nflaig
content: we also need to decide on https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/issues/599

author: pk910
content:
ah yea,  but that's for the local VC only?
it asks the BN for a locally built block with a self-bid.
then the stateful api can be used to submit it as the BN already knows about the self built block.

I'm not sure if something similar is worth doing for external builders.
In that case it's the builders job to broadcast the blobs to the network when revealing the payload. 
Those should be able to handle the stateless blob submission imo - they have higher hardware requirements and are well connected anyway..

author: pk910
content: `broadcast_validation=consensus_and_equivocation`  is a open gap in buildoor.  I defnitly gonna add it behind a setting

author: nflaig
content: so we are on the same page, I am mostly thinking about how prysm and lodestar for example would implement the builder though and whether it's worth to add apis for this on the spec side

author: nflaig
content:
> but that's for the local VC only?
well, not really the validator client, I really don't wanna mix builder paths into the vc, but a separate process like `lodestar builder`

author: nflaig
content: lodestar doesn't have the equivocation check implemented for payloads, we just log a warning that it's not there 😅

author: nflaig
content: but let's try to keep buildoor design close to how builders will work on mainnet later, so it's current workflow is already close too that

author: nflaig
content: how do you detect that your bid was chosen? and how do you decide to reveal or not?

author: nflaig
content: cc <@844595788550373406> <@921369499733725205> are working on `lodestar builder`, maybe we can use this channel to discuss topics, I don't wanna be to opinionated of how the builder works, I just wanna make sure we don't limit the design space due to gaps in the spec that can be easily resolved
